zh.lifeSummary: 當William de Torrington II於1134年出生在英國戴文格亞特 托林頓時，他的父親William de Torrington I25歲，母親Mable Tyldesley21歲。他和Baroness Mary of Torrington至少有1個兒子和2個女兒。他去世於1170年在他的家鄉享年36歲。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Torrington
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: 1  English: locative name, mainly from Black, Great, or Little Torrington (Devon).

2 English: in Lincs and E Anglia from East or West Torrington (Lincs), recorded as Terintone in 1086 and Tiringtuna about 1115, or from Terrington (Norfolk), recorded as Tirintuna between 1103 and 1131.

variants.name: Tarrington
meanings: 1  English: locative name, variant of   Torrington  .

2  English: locative name, perhaps sometimes from Tarrington (Herefs), but there is no clear link between the 1379 example and the 18th-century Staffs and Lancs name cited below, which may instead belong with (1).

variants.name: Terrington
meanings: 1  English: locative name, in S Lincs and E Anglia a variant of   Torrington   (2).
2  English: locative name in N England from Terrington (NR Yorks).

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

en.lifeSummary: When William de Torrington II was born in 1134, in Great Torrington, Devon, England, his father, William de Torrington I, was 25 and his mother, Mable Tyldesley, was 21. He had at least 1 son and 2 daughters with Baroness Mary of Torrington. He died in 1170, in his hometown, at the age of 36.

ja.lifeSummary: William de Torrington IIが1134年にイギリスデボングレアト トリントンで生まれた時，父William de Torrington Iは25歳，母Mable Tyldesleyは21歳であった。彼はBaroness Mary of Torringtonとの間に少なくとも1人の息子と2人の娘をもうけた。彼は1170年に故郷で36歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: William de Torrington II(이)가 1134년 영국 데폰 그리트 토링턴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 William de Torrington I(은)는 25세였고, 그의 어머니 Mable Tyldesley(은)는 21세였다. 그는 Baroness Mary of Torrington(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1170년 그의 고향에서 36세의 나이로 사망했다.
